title: Lukas Fink
text:
I am a PhD student in Economics at Freie Universität Berlin since 2023.
My research focuses on the Economics of Science, Public Economics, and issues of reproducibility and replicability. Currently, my research focuses on the state and impact of research openness.

Contact
l.fink[at]fu-berlin.de

title: Research
text:
Replication Code Availability Over Time and Across Fields: Evidence from the German Socio-Economic Panel
Economic Inquiry, 2025, 63(2), 357–386 (with Jan Marcus).
Open Access Publication · Replication Package
Free public transport: acceptance among the population and possible effects on mobility behavior (article in German)
List Forum für Wirtschafts- und Finanzpolitik, 2021, 46(3), 299–325 (with Mark A. Andor, Manuel Frondel, Andreas Gerster, and Marco Horvath).
Open Access Publication



Brodeur, A., et al. (2025). Comparing human-only, AI-assisted, and AI-led teams on assessing research reproducibility in quantitative social science.
The Institute for Replication (I4R) Discussion Paper Series, No. 195.
